title: Teaching
text:
2025: Teaching assistant and substitute lecturer for Applied Statistics (Bachelor); Seminar on Modern Economics (Bachelor)
2024: Teaching assistant for Applied Statistics (Bachelor); Seminar on Modern Economics (Bachelor); Introduction to Causal Inference (consultant outside university)
2023: Seminar on Causal Infernece (Master)

title: Aaron Lohmann
text:
I’m Aaron, an economics PhD candidate at IfW Kiel and Bielefeld University. My research focuses on the intersection of international trade, innovation, and digital technologies.
Download my CV here: Download CV

title: Research
text:
Work in progresss

More peers, more recombination but less exploration: Evidence from software developers – Draft coming soon.
When dispersed teams are more successful: Theory and evidence from software – with Gábor Békes, Julian Hinz, Miklós Koren – Draft coming soon.
Vibe Coding Kills Open Source – with Miklós Koren, Gábor Békes and Julian Hinz – Link to Paper
(Selected media coverage: Handelsblatt, The register 404 Media , Listen to Miklós discuss our paper and more on the podcast PracticalAI)
Code without Borders? Global Value Chains in Open Source Software Development – with Gábor Békes, Julian Hinz, Miklós Koren – Draft coming soon.




Policy Papers

America's Own Goal: Who Pays the Tarifs? – with Julian Hinz, Hendrik Mahlkow, Anna Vorwig – Link to Paper (Selected media coverage: Wall Street Journal, Die Zeit The Economist )




Conference Presentations/Seminars

2025: Rethink-GSC Workshop (Gdansk) , BiGSEM Seminar (Bielefeld) , EEA (Bordeaux) , ETSG (Milan)
2024: FIW (Vienna), BiGSEM Seminar (Bielefeld), EEA (Rotterdam), Kiel-Aarhus Workshop (Sandbjerg)
2023: BiGSEM Seminar (Bielefeld) , ETSG (Guildford), Firms and Productivity Workshop (Pescara) , Kiel-Aarhus Workshop (Sandbjerg)



Research visits

2026 (January - April): University of Toronto, Toronto, under supervision of Ruben Gaetani
2024 (February - June): Central European University, Vienna, under supervision of Miklós Koren

title: Code

R packages

narrSVAR R package to estimate SVAR models with sign restrictions and importantly narrtive sign restrictions as discussed in AR18.

title: Welcome!
text:
Welcome!
I’m Aaron, an economics PhD candidate at Kiel Institut and Bielefeld University. My research focuses on the intersection of international trade, innovation, and digital technologies.
You can find my CV here.
If you want to get in touch, feel free to email me: aaron.lohmann@kielinstitut.de
